figures.caption: Fig. 2: Synergy of the ordered hybrid method. Left: limitations of single-step pruning-only, quantization-only, and KD-only strategies, each optimizing mainly one axis (accuracy or efficiency). Right: our three-stage ordering, where pruning shrinks the active set, quantization converts sparsity into real efficiency gains, and KD recovers accuracy in the compressed space, moving the model toward the Pareto front in the accuracy–efficiency plane.
Fig. 4: Global analysis on ResNet-18/CIFAR-10: ROC/PR curves and the 3D accuracy–size–latency trade-off.

Fig. 1: Overview of the proposed hybrid compression pipeline. A dense FP32 teacher with weights W(0) is first pruned into a sparse FP32 student (W(p) = M⊙W(0)), then optimized under INT8 quantization (PTQ/QAT) to obtain a sparse INT8 student W(q) = Q8(W (p)), and finally refined via knowledge distillation to produce the hybrid student W(h) in the compressed space.
